audit_status.source_base: Greek Colossians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for fullness, philosophy/elemental powers, circumcision of Messiah, handwriting canceled, powers disarmed, food/festival judgment, ascetic severity.

verses.restored: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and those in Laodicea and as many as have not seen my face in flesh,
so that their hearts may be comforted/encouraged, being knit together in love and into all riches of the full assurance of understanding, into knowledge of the mystery of the Divine, Messiah,
in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
I say this so that no one may deceive you with persuasive speech.
For even if I am absent in flesh, yet in spirit I am with you, rejoicing and seeing your order and the firmness of your trust in Messiah.
Therefore, as you received Messiah Jesus the Lord, walk in him,
having been rooted and being built up in him and being confirmed in the trust, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
Watch lest anyone take you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental powers/principles of the world, and not according to Messiah.
Because in him all the fullness of deity dwells bodily,
and you have been filled in him, who is head of every ruler and authority.
In him also you were circumcised with a circumcision not made by hands, in the stripping off of the body of flesh, in the circumcision of Messiah,
having been buried with him in baptism, in whom also you were raised together through trust in the working of the Divine who raised him from the dead.
And you, being dead in trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, he made alive together with him, graciously forgiving us all trespasses,
having wiped out the handwriting against us in decrees that was opposed to us; he has taken it from the midst, nailing it to the cross.
Having stripped the rulers and authorities, he made a public display of them, triumphing over them in it.
Therefore let no one judge you in food or drink or in respect of festival or new moon or Sabbaths,
which are shadow of the things coming, but the body/substance belongs to Messiah.
Let no one disqualify you, delighting in humility and worship of messengers, entering into things one has seen, being puffed up without cause by the mind of one's flesh,
and not holding fast the head, from whom the whole body, through joints and ligaments being supplied and knit together, grows with the growth of the Divine.
If you died with Messiah from the elemental powers/principles of the world, why, as living in the world, do you submit to decrees:
Do not handle, nor taste, nor touch,
which are all into decay by use, according to human commandments and teachings?
These indeed have a word of wisdom in self-made religion and humility and severity to body, but are not of any value against fullness/satisfaction of the flesh.

translation_notes: Fullness of deity dwells bodily protects embodied fullness, not escape from embodiment.
Elemental powers/principles and human decrees are the concern, not embodied Jewish practice as such.
The food/festival/Sabbath passage should not be used as contempt for Jewish observance; the polemic concerns judgment/disqualification in this Colossian context.
